features.summary: Johnson & Johnson reported Q1 2025 worldwide sales of $21.9 billion with 4.2% operational growth despite an 810 basis point headwind from STELARA biosimilar competition. Innovative Medicine grew 4.2% to $13.9 billion while MedTech grew 4.1% to $8 billion. The company maintained full-year guidance with operational sales growth of 3.3-4.3% and adjusted EPS of $10.50-$10.70. Key growth drivers included DARZALEX (22.5% growth), Tremfya (20.1% growth), and strong performance from acquired assets Abiomed and Shockwave.
features.management_outlook: Management expressed confidence in 2025 as a 'catalyst year' that will set up accelerated growth through the second half of the decade. They expect both Innovative Medicine and MedTech operational sales growth to be higher in the second half versus first half. The company completed the $14.1 billion acquisition of IntraCellular Therapies and announced a $55 billion four-year U.S. investment plan. Management projects several pipeline assets will achieve significantly higher sales than current street estimates, with riboflavin plus LASCRUZ expected to be at least 2x higher than 2027 consensus.
features.risks: The company faces continued STELARA biosimilar erosion expected to accelerate throughout 2025 following the HUMIRA erosion curve, combined with Part D redesign impacts. Tariff exposure includes an estimated $400 million impact in 2025, primarily affecting MedTech operations with limited pricing flexibility to offset costs. MedTech faces competitive pressures in electrophysiology, orthopedics performance below market, and one-time revenue disruptions from transformation initiatives. The talc litigation returned to the tort system after bankruptcy plan rejection.
features.key_drivers: Growth is driven by oncology portfolio strength with DARZALEX maintaining over 20% growth and expanding indications, plus strong launches of CARVYKTI, TECVAYLI, and Talvey. Tremfya's expansion into IBD markets shows significant momentum with fastest growth in ulcerative colitis and strong early Crohn's disease uptake. The riboflavin plus LASCRUZ combination demonstrated over one-year overall survival improvement in EGFR-mutated lung cancer. Pipeline catalysts include expected 2025 approvals for Caplyta in major depressive disorder, subcutaneous riboflavin, and Ichotrochindra filing in psoriasis.
